summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: How the Visa-Mastercard card fee case may end
articles.description: A federal court is likely to weigh how much a proposed settlement over interchange fees differs from a pact that was rejected two years ago.

articles.title: FIS, Barclays extend core banking tie
articles.description: The payments processor has won a multi-year extension of its core banking technology contract with the British financial institution.

articles.title: The business case for blockchain in B2B payments
articles.description: Increased regulatory clarity and the rise of stablecoin infrastructure have transformed how digital assets are used.

articles.title: Tapping into the latest emerging payment solution trends
articles.description: Strategically integrating these solutions can help deliver the seamless shopping customers demand.

articles.title: How EWA improves workers’ lives
articles.description: Earned wage access “users are savvy managers of their money who value EWA products, use them responsibly, and have positive experiences,” writes a trade group leader.

articles.title: Amex to buy Hypercard
articles.description: The card company has agreed to purchase the agentic expense management firm backed by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman.

articles.title: U.S. Bank eyes Amazon tie-up for small-biz boost
articles.description: The lender aims to pitch banking services to the 700,000 small-business owners it will gain exposure to through a credit card partnership with Amazon.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard settle merchant claims
articles.description: About 65 retailers suing the networks over card swipe fees in a New York case have now settled claims.
